title: H·AI·K·U Intent Frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter on .haiku/intents/<slug>/intent.md. Distinguishes user-writable fields (settable via haiku_intent_set) from engine-managed fields (mutated only by the workflow engine).

studio.description: Studio (lifecycle template) name. Immutable after creation.

mode.description: Execution mode. Engine-managed via haiku_select_mode — never set directly by the agent.

autopilot.description: Legacy autopilot flag. Use `mode: autopilot` instead. Engine-managed during migration.

active_stage.description: Currently active stage. Engine-managed (state.json is the source of truth).

stages.description: Stage list (allow-list for /haiku:quick scope). Set on creation.

skip_stages.description: Stages to skip in this intent.

intent_reviewed.description: Whether the elaborated intent passed initial review. Engine-managed.

intent_completion_review.description: Whether to run studio-level completion review at the end of the final stage. Defaults to true.

completion_review_dispatched.description: Whether studio-level review agents have been dispatched. Engine-managed.

completion_review_skipped.description: True when no studio-level review agents are configured. Engine-managed.

completion_review_entered_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the intent entered completion review. Engine-managed.

completion_review_dispatched_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp review agents were dispatched. Engine-managed.

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the intent sealed. Engine-managed.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the intent was created. Immutable.

archived.description: Whether the intent is archived. Toggle via haiku_intent_archive / haiku_intent_unarchive.

archived_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the intent was archived. Engine-managed.

follows.description: Slug of the parent intent this one follows from. Set on creation.

composite.description: Composite-intent topology. Either an array of {studio, stages} entries (creation-time spec) or true (already-elaborated marker).
